events.source_excerpt: Strait of Hormuz: Effectively closed to most commercial traffic. Iran allowing selective passage. First transit (Pakistani tanker) on March 16.

events.title: Lapid Files No-Confidence Motion Against Netanyahu Government
events.description: Israeli opposition leader Yair Lapid filed a no-confidence motion on March 16, 2026, as cracks in the wartime opposition unity emerged after the coalition resumed advancing controversial legislation during wartime. Democrats leader Yair Golan became the first major opposition figure to challenge war policy.
events.source_excerpt: Lapid filed a no-confidence motion March 16. Democrats leader Yair Golan became the first major opposition figure to challenge war policy: 'This government does not know how to win wars.'

events.title: USS Gerald R. Ford Experiences Fire Onboard
events.description: USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) temporarily experienced a fire onboard on March 17, 2026, while deployed to the Red Sea.
events.source_excerpt: Temporarily experienced fire onboard March 17.

events.title: Ali Larijani and Basij Commander Gholamreza Soleimani Killed
events.description: Ali Larijani, Supreme National Security Council Secretary, and Gholamreza Soleimani, Basij Commander, were killed in US-Israeli strikes on March 17, 2026, further depleting Iranian senior leadership.
events.source_excerpt: Ali Larijani, Supreme National Security Council Secretary, March 17; Gholamreza Soleimani, Basij Commander, March 17.

events.title: Trump Reverses Course on Allied Help; Threatens to Leave NATO
events.description: After being rebuffed by NATO allies who refused to participate in the conflict, Trump reversed course on March 17–18, stating 'We don't need any help, actually' and threatening to leave NATO. US Energy Secretary Chris Wright also stated the US was 'not ready' to escort tankers through the Strait.
events.source_excerpt: Trump's reaction (March 17–18): After being rebuffed, Trump reversed course: 'We don't need any help, actually.' He threatened to leave NATO.

events.title: 13 US Service Members Killed as of March 17
events.description: As of March 17, 2026, 13 US service members had been killed in the conflict — 7 by enemy fire and 6 in a KC-135 Stratotanker crash in western Iraq. Over 200 had been wounded across seven countries, with approximately 180 returned to duty and 8 remaining severely injured.
events.source_excerpt: 13 US service members killed as of March 17 (7 by enemy fire, 6 in a KC-135 Stratotanker crash in western Iraq). 200+ wounded across seven countries.

events.title: Joe Kent Resigns as NCTC Director Over Iran War
events.description: Joe Kent resigned as Director of the National Counterterrorism Center on March 17, 2026, becoming the highest-ranking Trump administration official to resign over the Iran war. His resignation letter stated that Iran posed no imminent threat and the war was started due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby.
events.source_excerpt: Confirmed: Joe Kent resigned as Director of the National Counterterrorism Center (NCTC) on March 17, 2026 — the highest-ranking Trump administration official to resign over the Iran war... 'I cannot in good conscience support the ongoing war in Iran. Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation, and it is clear that we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby.'

events.title: House Oversight Committee Subpoenas AG Bondi Over Epstein Files
events.description: The House Oversight Committee voted on a bipartisan basis on March 17, 2026 — with 5 Republicans joining — to subpoena Attorney General Bondi over the Epstein files. Rep. Massie suggested Bondi could face obstruction charges. DOJ was caught logging what congressional members were searching in the files.
events.source_excerpt: AG Bonni was subpoenaed on March 17 by the House Oversight Committee (bipartisan vote, 5 Republicans joining). Rep. Massie suggested Bondi could face obstruction charges. DOJ was caught logging what congressional members were searching in the files — described as 'a form of intimidation.'

events.title: Russia Expands Intelligence Sharing with Iran to Include Improved Drone Guidance
events.description: The Wall Street Journal reported on March 17 that Russia had expanded its intelligence sharing with Iran to include improved drone guidance enabling more precise strikes on radar and missile systems in the Gulf, building on the initial confirmation of intelligence sharing reported on March 6.
events.source_excerpt: By March 17, the Wall Street Journal reported Russia had expanded intelligence sharing to include improved drone guidance enabling 'more precise strikes on radar and missile systems in the Gulf.'

events.title: UAE Gas Field Set Ablaze in Iranian Strike
events.description: A UAE gas field was set ablaze on March 17 in an Iranian strike, part of a broader pattern of Iranian attacks on Gulf state energy infrastructure including the Mussafah Fuel Terminal and Fujairah Oil Terminal.
events.source_excerpt: a separate UAE gas field (set ablaze March 17) were all hit.

events.title: US National Average Gasoline Price Reaches $3.79/Gallon — Up 30% in One Month
events.description: The US national average gasoline price reached $3.79 per gallon as of March 17, up $0.87 (+30%) from $2.92 one month prior. Diesel hit $5.04/gallon, up $1.34. California gas reached $5.34–5.48/gallon.
events.source_excerpt: The national average gasoline price reached $3.79/gallon as of March 17 — up $0.87 (+30%) from $2.92 one month prior. Diesel hit $5.04/gallon, up $1.34.

events.title: Israel Strikes South Pars Natural Gas Field and Neighboring Refineries
events.description: Israel struck South Pars natural gas field — the world's largest shared gas field, shared with Qatar — and neighboring refineries on March 18, 2026, representing a significant escalation in targeting energy infrastructure with potential spillover effects on Qatar.
events.source_excerpt: March 18 — Israel struck South Pars natural gas field (world's largest shared gas field with Qatar) and neighboring refineries.

events.title: Intelligence Minister Esmaeil Khatib Killed
events.description: Iranian Intelligence Minister Esmaeil Khatib was killed in US-Israeli strikes on March 18, 2026, further decapitating Iran's intelligence apparatus.
events.source_excerpt: Esmaeil Khatib, Intelligence Minister, March 18.

events.title: US Strikes Iranian Anti-Ship Missile Launchers Along Strait of Hormuz Coast
events.description: The US struck Iranian anti-ship missile launchers along the Strait of Hormuz coastline using bunker-buster bombs on March 18, 2026, as part of efforts to reopen the Strait to commercial traffic.
events.source_excerpt: The US has struck Iranian anti-ship missile launchers along the Strait of Hormuz coast using bunker-buster bombs on March 18.

events.title: CENTCOM Reports 7,000+ Targets Struck in Iran Since February 28
events.description: As of March 16–18, 2026, CENTCOM reported striking 7,000+ targets in Iran since February 28. Defense Secretary Hegseth had separately claimed 15,000+ targets by March 14, including some from Israeli operations.
events.source_excerpt: As of March 16, CENTCOM reported striking 7,000+ targets in Iran since February 28. Defense Secretary Hegseth claimed 15,000+ targets by March 14 (including some from Israeli operations).

events.title: Brent Crude Oil Peaks at $126/Barrel; Currently Trading at ~$103
events.description: Brent crude oil surged from approximately $65/barrel before the war to a peak of $126/barrel, currently trading at approximately $103/barrel — a 40–50% increase driven by the Strait of Hormuz closure and attacks on regional energy infrastructure.
events.source_excerpt: Brent crude surged from ~$65/barrel pre-war to $126/barrel peak, currently trading ~$103/barrel — a 40–50% increase.

events.title: Regional Death Toll Reaches 2,300+ as War Continues with No Ceasefire
events.description: As of approximately March 17–18, 2026 (Day 19 of the 2026 war), approximately 2,300+ people had been killed regionally: 1,444 in Iran, 886+ in Lebanon, 17+ in Israel, 21+ in Gulf states, 58+ in Iraq, and 13 US service members. No ceasefire was in sight.
events.source_excerpt: ~2,300+ killed regionally: 1,444 in Iran (Health Ministry; independent monitors estimate higher), 886+ in Lebanon, 17+ in Israel, 21+ in Gulf states, 58+ in Iraq, 13 US service members.

events.title: Brent Crude at $108.78 per Barrel on Day 19 of Iran War
events.description: Brent crude oil was trading at $108.78 per barrel on March 18, 2026 — Day 19 of the Iran war — representing a 55%+ increase from the pre-war price of approximately $70 per barrel.
events.source_excerpt: Brent crude surged from ~$70/bbl pre-war to $108.78/bbl on March 18 — a 55%+ increase.

events.title: Fox News Mid-March Poll Shows 50-50 Republican-Base Split on Iran War
events.description: A Fox News poll conducted in mid-March 2026 found 50% support and 50% opposition to the Iran war, reflecting the fracturing of the Republican base on the conflict.
events.source_excerpt: Fox News (mid-Mar) | 50% | 50% | —

events.title: Israel Strikes South Pars Gas Processing Facility Phases 3–6
events.description: Israel struck the South Pars gas processing facility phases 3–6 on March 18, taking offline portions of the world's largest natural gas field. This represented a significant escalation in targeting Iranian energy infrastructure.
events.source_excerpt: most significantly, Israel struck the South Pars gas processing facility (phases 3–6) on March 18, taking offline portions of the world's largest gas field.

events.title: Brent Crude Reaches $108.78/bbl — Up Approximately 47–68% from Pre-Conflict Baseline
events.description: Brent crude stood at $108.78 per barrel on March 18, climbing to $109.95 intraday (+6.3% on the day alone), representing an increase of approximately 47–68% from the pre-conflict baseline of $65–73/bbl depending on which baseline is used.
events.source_excerpt: Brent crude stood at $108.78/bbl on March 18, 2026, climbing to $109.95 intraday (+6.3% on the day alone). Pre-conflict Brent traded at $65–73/bbl.

events.title: Iran's IRGC Threatens Five Specific Gulf Facilities with Imminent Strikes
events.description: Iran's IRGC threatened to strike five specific Gulf facilities within hours on March 18: the SAMREF refinery and Al-Jubail petrochemical complex in Saudi Arabia, the Al-Hosn gas field in the UAE, and both the Mesaieed complex and Ras Laffan refinery in Qatar.
events.source_excerpt: On March 18, Iran's IRGC threatened to strike five specific Gulf facilities within hours: the SAMREF refinery and Al-Jubail petrochemical complex in Saudi Arabia, the Al-Hosn gas field in the UAE, and both the Mesaieed complex and Ras Laffan refinery in Qatar.

events.title: Critical Israeli Budget Deadline — Knesset Dissolution If Budget Fails
events.description: The Israeli 2026 state budget must pass its second and third Knesset readings by March 31, 2026, or the Knesset automatically dissolves, triggering elections approximately 90 days later. The first reading passed 53–45.
events.source_excerpt: Critical deadline: March 31, 2026. The 2026 state budget must pass second and third readings by this date or the Knesset automatically dissolves, triggering elections ~90 days later. The first reading passed 53–45.

events.title: 2026 US Midterm Elections — Projected Republican Losses of ~28 House Seats
events.description: Pre-war structural models projected Republicans losing approximately 28 House seats in the November 3, 2026 midterms — enough to lose the chamber. Sabato's Crystal Ball identified 29 Republican-held seats in jeopardy. The Iran war compounds pre-existing vulnerabilities with economic anxiety, high gas prices, and base fracture.
events.source_excerpt: Pre-war structural models projected Republicans losing ~28 House seats — enough to lose the chamber. Sabato's Crystal Ball identified 29 Republican-held seats in jeopardy... Sen. Rand Paul warned: if oil prices stay high, 'you're going to see a disastrous election' for the GOP.
